# Repository Objectives

## Purpose
Document the repository's evidence-backed purpose here after inspecting its code, configuration, documentation, issues, and workflows.

## Intended users and use cases
- Identify primary users.
- Identify the problems or workflows the repository supports.
- Separate current capabilities from planned capabilities.

## Objectives
| Objective | Evidence | Success measure | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Establish the repository's actual purpose and scope | README, manifests, source tree, issues | Purpose statement matches implementation and active plans | Pending |
| Map components and interfaces | Source, configuration, APIs, workflows | Component map identifies entry points and dependencies | Pending |
| Convert goals into measurable work | Existing tasks, issues, comments, roadmap | Each active objective has acceptance criteria | Pending |
| Validate quality and safety | Tests, CI, security configuration, release process | Validation gaps are documented and prioritized | Pending |

## Constraints and non-goals
Record architectural, licensing, data, security, compatibility, resource, and governance constraints. Explicitly state what the repository does not intend to do.

## Open questions
List only questions that materially affect scope, architecture, safety, or completion criteria.
